item.text: I’ll fix the reviewed `/fork` runtime issue specifically. The problem is not slash routing anymore; it is the Codex backend invoking an interactive top-level command through piped stdio, so I’m going to make `/fork` produce backend output without that broken piped interactive path and add a spawned-Codex regression test for it.

The reviewer’s concrete failure is the spawned Codex `/fork` path, so I’m checking whether the repo already has a non-interactive fork concept separate from Codex’s TUI command before choosing the smallest safe fix.

item.text: The repo’s existing “fork” UI action is only a UI action placeholder, not a backend implementation. Since top-level Codex fork is interactive/stateful and unsuitable for Work Leaf’s piped backend command runner, I’m going to make Codex `/fork` a non-model backend response that reports the selected thread and explains that non-interactive fork is unavailable instead of spawning the broken command.
I’m removing the Codex top-level fork invocation entirely. `/fork` will be handled as backend command output from `CodexBackend` state, so it cannot hit the interactive CLI path under piped stdio.
Next I’m changing the command dispatch so `/fork` renders that non-interactive backend output and records it as a normal agent command result, rather than building any process invocation.

The dispatch now uses an `unsupported_backend_command` helper for unknown slash tokens. I’m adding that helper locally so unknown commands still fail as backend commands without falling back to prompts.

I’m updating the Codex unit test so it proves `/fork` returns backend output instead of proving the old broken argv shape. Then I’ll add the spawned-Codex regression test for “do not invoke top-level fork.”

The spawned-Codex test will now send both `/status` and `/fork`. The fake Codex binary emits a failure marker if either command reaches `exec resume` or top-level `fork`, which catches the reviewed regression.
The architecture doc still says `/fork` uses a Codex fork invocation. I’m correcting that to describe the non-interactive backend output behavior.
The behavior, tests, and docs are updated for the `/fork` fix. I’m formatting before running the focused regression tests.
Formatting completed. I’m running the Codex slash unit test and the terminal slash tests that now cover the spawned-Codex `/fork` regression.

item.text:
Implemented the reviewer fix.

`/fork` no longer invokes the interactive top-level `codex fork` path through piped stdio. In [src/codex.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-2/src/codex.rs), Codex backend slash handling now renders non-interactive backend fork output for the selected agent/thread, matching the chat-command execution model.

I also tightened coverage in [tests/terminal_app.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-2/tests/terminal_app.rs) so `/fork` would fail if it reached the interactive fake `codex fork` path, and updated the Codex backend unit test to assert the rendered fork output.

Verification passed:
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets --all-features`
- Real Codex smoke command succeeded and returned `WORK_LEAF_REAL_AGENT_OK`

Changes are left uncommitted.
